days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring vibrant city life

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Land at Hong Kong International Airport and make your way to the city via the Airport Express train for a quick and efficient transfer.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Icon
timeline.detail: Settle into your luxurious stay at Hotel Icon, just a short walk from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station. Known for its stylish decor and excellent service.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Indulge in the world's cheapest Michelin-starred dim sum at Tim Ho Wan. Try their signature baked buns and prawn dumplings for a delightful experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Peak
timeline.detail: Take the Peak Tram up to Victoria Peak for stunning views of Hong Kong's skyline and Victoria Harbour. Explore the walking trails and shops at the peak.

timeline.title: Dinner at Aqua Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a romantic dinner overlooking the harbor at Aqua Restaurant. Known for its contemporary Asian cuisine and breathtaking views of the skyline.
timeline.mapQuery: Aqua Restaurant, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: A Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Catch the spectacular light show featuring lasers and lights from buildings on both sides of Victoria Harbour. Best viewed from the Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Waterfront, Hong Kong

localTips: Get an Octopus Card at the airport for convenient travel on public transport.
Consider making dinner reservations at popular restaurants to avoid long waits.
Try to visit local markets in Mong Kok for authentic shopping.

days.title: Cultural Exploration
days.subtitle: Delving into history and culture

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a traditional Hong Kong breakfast at Australia Dairy Company. Try their toast with scrambled eggs and signature milk tea.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple, famous for its fortune telling and beautiful architecture. Spend some time walking through the serene grounds and absorbing the atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Savor some of the best wonton noodles in Hong Kong at Mak's Noodle. Known for its thin noodles and fresh ingredients, be prepared for a delicious meal.

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Discover Hong Kong's rich heritage at the Hong Kong Museum of History. Learn about the city's fascinating past through engaging exhibits and artifacts.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Stroll around Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely walk along Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, take in the iconic skyline views, and relax before dinner.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Charcoal Grill
timeline.detail: Wrap up your day with a delightful meal at Charcoal Grill, known for its steaks and vibrant atmosphere. Make sure to try their signature dishes.
timeline.mapQuery: Charcoal Grill, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

highlights: Fortune telling at Wong Tai Sin
localTips: Purchase a museum pass for discounts if you plan to visit multiple museums.
Try to avoid peak hours when visiting popular tourist sites.
Carry cash, especially for local eateries.

days.title: Shopping and Leisure
days.subtitle: A day of markets and final explorations

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delightful breakfast at Hotel Icon. Take advantage of their extensive buffet featuring both Western and Asian dishes.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Ladies' Market
timeline.detail: Head to Ladies' Market in Mong Kok where you'll find a variety of fashion, accessories, and souvenirs. Perfect for some last-minute shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies' Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at a Local Eatery
timeline.detail: Find a local eatery near the market and try some street food specialties like fish balls or egg waffles to get a real taste of Hong Kong.
timeline.mapQuery: Local Eatery, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Nan Lian Garden
timeline.detail: Take a peaceful afternoon stroll through Nan Lian Garden. This beautifully landscaped garden offers a quiet escape from the bustling city.
timeline.mapQuery: Nan Lian Garden, Diamond Hill, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Check Out
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Icon, freshen up, and prepare for departure. Make sure to collect all your luggage and belongings.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Transfer to Airport
timeline.detail: Make your way back to Hong Kong International Airport by Airport Express for your flight back home, reflecting on the lovely time spent in the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau, Hong Kong

localTips: Bargain at street markets for the best prices, especially in Ladies' Market.
Plan your airport transfer in advance to avoid last-minute rush.
Check the market schedules as some may close early on certain days.

hotels.name: Hotel Icon
hotels.note: Luxury hotel near Tsim Sha Tsui, excellent service
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong
